steps.title: Simplify and interpret the exponent
steps.content: Rewrite: $\frac{8}{6}t = \frac{4}{3}t$. Since $t$ is in years, the coefficient $\frac{4}{3}$ represents the number of 3% growth periods per year.

steps.title: Find the period in months
steps.content:
If growth of 3% occurs every $n$ months, then in 12 months (1 year), there are $\frac{12}{n}$ periods. This must equal $\frac{4}{3}$:
$$\frac{12}{n} = \frac{4}{3}$$
$$n = 12 \div \frac{4}{3} = 9$$
That matches Choice A — $9$.
